title: Rickshaw Urdu Phrases 🇵🇰
text: So, here's how to hire a rickshaw here in Karachi, in Pakistan. The first thing you need to know is the landmark you're going to, whereabouts you're going. You don't quote roads here. You don't quote quote addresses like we do. You need a landmark. So, I'm going to eat biryani at Allah Wala Biryani's. So, that's what I'm going to say to the guy. And like another good landmark would be KFC. KFC and then the area name. This is KFC Nursery. And then let's see what the driver asks us for after. And there'll be a little bit of bargaining. You'll see. Okay, so there's rickshaws everywhere, right? These are all rickshaws you can approach. Ideally, if they don't have someone in them. As-salamu alaykum, ji. Okay, sir. Mujhe um Allah Wala Biryani jana hai. Yes. Kitne rupay? Dhai sau, 250. 250, okay. Chale. If you know a little bit of Urdu or Hindi, it really helps here. But just say where you're going and ask how much. They will know enough English to understand how much. And you can bargain if you want. You should bargain cuz um [music] they're giving the foreigner rate. And one more thing, the people right in front of me, they are motorcycle rickshaws. So, you can take a motorcycle for way cheaper as well, but you know, more dangerous, too.
